title: Office of Tax Analysis
summary: This is the Treasury Department's Office of Tax Analysis (OTA) landing page, which serves as an index to tax policy research, distributional analyses, and technical papers. The page contains links to tax burden data, tax expenditure analyses, OECD effective tax rate comparisons, child credit distribution data, health tax provisions, and various working papers. It is a navigation/listing page, not a specific research document or policy announcement.
key_points: OTA provides distributional analysis of U.S. tax burden under current law, with data files from 2015-2025
The page links to OECD effective marginal tax rates (EMTRs) and effective average tax rates (EATRs) for corporate investment across member countries for years 2017-2023
Tax expenditure distribution analyses include estate and gift tax exemptions, retirement tax benefits, and premium tax credits
Child Tax Credit advance payment disbursement records from 2021 are archived with monthly state-level breakdowns
Health tax provision analyses include ACA Marketplace coverage data and employer-sponsored insurance tax exclusion estimates
Technical papers referenced include Methodology Technical Paper 8 (distribution methodology) and Technical Paper 9/10 (effective tax rate calculations)
why_it_matters: This page is a reference index to Treasury's tax analysis function. It does not itself contain market-moving announcements, policy decisions, or actionable macro data. The linked research documents could be relevant for fiscal policy analysis but are not direct market catalysts.
notes: NON-ARCHIVEABLE: This is a navigation/listing page for Treasury's Office of Tax Analysis. Per CORE RULE 6, homepage, navigation_page, and listing_page types should be treated as non-archiveable. The page contains structural links to various tax analysis documents and data files but does not itself constitute a market-relevant document, policy decision, or macro catalyst. The individual linked documents (distributional analyses, working papers, technical papers) may have separate archival value if assessed individually. The OTA research topics covered (tax burden distribution, tax expenditures, OECD tax rate comparisons) are fiscal policy research rather than direct market catalysts or market structure content.

reasoning: Source is a navigation page, not a standalone document
No specific publication date for the page
Contains only structural links to other documents
Not relevant to market structure or actionable macro catalysts
